# Lyrd

Lyrd is a React overlay intent system. It centrally manages overlay requests, typed results, lifecycle, deduplication, and scheduling policy while the application owns JSX, styling, copy, and UI primitive adapters.

Documentation: https://seunjin.github.io/lyrd/
Complete agent documentation: https://seunjin.github.io/lyrd/llms-full.txt
Ready-to-use integration test prompt: https://seunjin.github.io/lyrd/agent-test-prompt.md
Repository: https://github.com/seunjin/lyrd
Packages: @lyrd/core and @lyrd/cli

## Start here

1. Read `llms-full.txt` before changing an application.
2. Use `pnpm dlx @lyrd/cli@next add overlay --verbose` to generate app-owned Base UI renderers.
3. Mount the generated Provider exactly once at the app root.
4. Use `alert()` and `confirm()` for safe built-in recipes.
5. Use `defineOverlay()` and `open()` for reusable custom overlays.
6. Retain the awaitable handle returned by `open()` when one flow must call `update()` or `dismiss()` on that exact session.
7. Use `openOrUpdate()` only when multiple call sites must find one active operation by a stable identity.
8. Use an explicit parallel group for non-blocking overlays such as Toast.

There is no built-in `overlay.toast()` API. Use `pnpm dlx @lyrd/cli@next add toast` and keep the generated adapter in the application.
